body:
Worried about memory and mental clarity?

Many people experiencing brain fog and memory issues often don't realize the impact until it becomes a serious concern.

That’s why I just put together a brand new video that shows how to help: 

✅ Improve Mental Clarity

There's a quiet kind of inflammation that affects memory. Here's what helps.

Most of us picture inflammation as a sore knee or a cold. But scientists now talk about "inflammaging," a low, steady inflammation that builds with age and quietly affects the brain. The hippocampus, the part you lean on for memory, is one of the most sensitive areas. The encouraging part is that much of it comes down to small daily choices you actually control.

A simple routine that helps:
1. Move about 30 minutes most days. A walk counts.
2. Take 10 minutes to slow your breathing and ease stress.
3. Build meals around leafy greens and omega-3s like fish, nuts, and seeds, and go easy on processed sugar.
4. Protect your sleep with a calm wind-down before bed.
5. Add protein and fiber to each plate to keep blood sugar steady.

None of this is dramatic, and that is the point. These habits gently lower the daily inflammation that wears on memory over time. Dr. Sam Walters built Youthful Brain to work alongside routines like these. As always, talk with your doctor before starting anything new.

Want to keep your mind in top shape? Watch my video for the science behind it!  

#BrainHealth #MemoryMatters #SleepScience #HealthyAging #SelfImprovement #LearnWithMe

Two elephants spent a single winter together in a circus back in the 1970s — Jenny just a calf newly arrived from Asia, Shirley already full-grown. Then they were separated for more than two decades.

In 1999 they were reunited at a sanctuary in Tennessee. Nobody had to introduce them. They bellowed, strained toward each other, and pushed so hard against the steel bars between them that the bars actually bent. By morning, Shirley had taken Jenny under her wing like a mother — picking up right where they'd left off 20+ years before.

Their reunion became an Emmy-winning documentary, The Urban Elephant. And it's a reminder of something simple: memory is one of the most remarkable things a brain does — in elephants and in us. Keeping it sharp comes down to the basics, like steady, healthy blood flow feeding the brain.

Follow for more true stories about how memory works. 🐘

Your brain is just 2% of your body weight — but it demands about 20% of your oxygen.
 
That's why it leans so heavily on steady, oxygen-rich blood flow. And here's the part most people are never told: circulation naturally changes with age. By 70, blood flow to the brain can be measurably lower than it was at 40. Less flow means less of the oxygen and fuel your memory regions depend on — which can show up as mental fatigue or slower recall.
 
It's one reason ingredients studied for healthy circulation, like Ginkgo, get so much attention in brain-health research.
 
Follow — we're walking through one brain system at a time.

MSG is hiding in your food right now — under seven different names you've probably never heard. And it's doing something to your brain that the food industry doesn't want you to know.

I'm Dr. Sam Walters, brain health specialist and former NASA nutritional scientist. In 48 years of clinical practice, I've watched MSG do more damage to aging brains than almost any other single ingredient.

Here's why it's so dangerous.

MSG belongs to a category of chemicals scientists call "excitotoxins." That's not marketing language — that's the technical term. Excitotoxins work by overstimulating your brain cells, firing them at such an intense rate that the cells literally exhaust themselves and die. Once those neurons are gone, they don't grow back.

For decades, MSG was openly listed on labels. But as more research came out, consumers started looking for it and avoiding it. So the food industry did what it always does. It didn't remove the chemical. It just renamed it.

Today, if you see any of the following on an ingredient label, you're looking at MSG in disguise. Get a pen and write these down.

Hydrolyzed protein. Autolyzed yeast extract. Natural flavoring. Yeast extract. Soy protein isolate. Sodium caseinate. Disodium inosinate.

Seven names. One chemical. And it's likely in your kitchen right now.

In fact, in my clinical practice I'd estimate ninety percent of the patients I see over the age of 50 have at least two MSG-containing products in their pantry — and almost none of them know it. They're eating it in soups, sauces, salad dressings, frozen meals, even foods marketed as organic or "all natural."
And MSG is just one of five chemicals I now call the Brain Killers.

The other four include a sweetener that's chemically similar to a pesticide, a flavor compound that crosses the blood-brain barrier and creates Alzheimer's-style plaques, a heavy metal that's in your drinking water at concentrations 100 times higher than it was for your grandparents, and one more substance that may be the most insidious of them all because it's marketed as "diet" and "healthy."

The good news is this: even after years of exposure, the brain has a remarkable ability to detoxify and repair itself when given the right nutritional support. I've seen patients in their 70s and 80s regain memory function they thought was gone forever.
If you want to see the full list of all five Brain Killers — and the simple natural protocol I've developed to help your brain detoxify and rebuild — tap the button below right now.

title: Straight From the Warehouse
body:
Straight from the warehouse.

I don't usually write these. I run shipping out of one of our 4Patriots warehouses — we have two, Salt Lake City and Atlanta. My boss told me to explain what's been showing up at our dock lately.

Customers have been mailing us filters. Real ones, they thought. Counterfeit ones, it turns out. Some of them came in boxes that looked almost identical to the real category-leading brand's packaging. Same color. Same font. Same certification logos printed on the side. One guy sent us three filters he'd used over 18 months. None of them were what the box said they were.

Here's what NMCL — the parent company of that category-leading brand — doesn't want in the news. They filed a class action: Case 3:24-cv-01106. Their own lab testing found 89% of replacement filters sold under their brand name online in 2024 were counterfeit. 46 sellers named. It's all public record.

People started calling us because they wanted to know if OUR filter was legitimate.

Here's what I can tell you from the warehouse floor.

4Patriots is the exclusive worldwide source of genuine Nanomesh gravity-fed filters. The filters themselves are manufactured in Nevada. Finished units ship direct from our warehouses in Salt Lake City and Atlanta — I see the pallets, I see the certification paperwork. Every filter traces back to IAPMO Certificate W-16355, publicly listed on the NSF registry under 4Patriots.

We don't sell on Amazon. We don't use third-party resellers. We don't license the brand. The only way to get a real Nanomesh is direct from us. That's not a marketing choice. That's how we make sure what's on the box is what's in the box.

The Only U.S.-Made Triple NSF Certified gravity filter — NSF/ANSI 42, 53, 401. Targets 200+ contaminants. Reduces lead, PFAS, chlorine, pharmaceuticals. Zero silver. 90-day money-back guarantee. 60-second install.

The independent testing lab at IAPMO R&T called it "the best gravity filter tested at this lab in 18 years of operations."

Bronze 5-Pack $297. Gold 10-Pack $577. Diamond 15-Pack $797 — as low as $53 per filter. Free shipping.
